strands.title: Task Execution & Polling Pattern
strands.content: Three sequential bash task submissions (bnk191til, b583n243e, and b5mvz85yy) remained in 'running' state across multiple polling cycles until cycle 68. Task b583n243e COMPLETED with exit code 0 at cycle 68. Significant semantic shift: assistant transitions from polling status updates to ANALYSIS mode, then to BACKGROUND TASK MONITORING. The completed task output reveals batched tensor projection experiment across 6 cycles (turns 1-30), with structured strand, claim, and loss accounting. Task execution has proven viable for generating detailed comparative projections.

key_claims.text: Task b583n243e completed successfully with exit code 0, producing batched tensor projections for turns 1-30
Batched projection experiment produced 6 tensor cycles with increasing strand/claim counts but decreasing epistemic confidence in later cycles (T/I/F collapse to 0.0)

strands.title: Tensor Compression Empirical Results - Validated Trend
strands.content: Empirical validation: compression ratio genuinely improves as conversation length increases. Raw context exhibits O(n) growth (94KB → 357KB across 10 turns, 3.8x growth). Tensor remains bounded in 12-18KB range, achieving sublinear scaling. Final 21.7x compression ratio at turn 10 reflects asymptotic behavior — semantic state approaches ~16-18KB ceiling independent of further raw context accumulation. Turn 6 anomaly (246 bytes, 1395.7x ratio) is now recontextualized as OUTLIER/WIPEOUT event, not representative of trend. The trend itself is robust: tensor projection achieves meaningful compression through semantic consolidation, not serialization artifact. Haiku-based naive per-turn strategy (first attempt, unoptimized) already demonstrates sublinear behavior.

key_claims.text: Raw context grows O(n) with turns; tensor remains O(1) bounded, yielding improving compression ratio as conversation lengthens
Tensor approaches ~16-18KB ceiling, suggesting natural semantic state size limit for coding conversations independent of transcript length
Turn 6 collapse (246 bytes) is measured outlier/reset event, not representative of compression methodology
21.7x final compression reflects genuine semantic consolidation, not serialization noise

strands.title: Batched Projection Experiment - Cycle-by-Cycle Analysis
strands.content: Task b583n243e executed batched tensor projection on turns 1-30, spanning 6 cycles (5 turns per cycle). CYCLE 1 (turns 1-5, 1198 chars): 3 strands, 11 claims, 3 losses, ~1718 tokens, T=0.65 I=0.30 F=0.05. CYCLE 2 (turns 6-10, 677 chars): 4 strands, 16 claims, 4 losses, ~3085 tokens, T=0.62 I=0.32 F=0.06. CYCLES 3-6 (turns 11-30): epistemic values collapse to T=0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0, suggesting loss of epistemic grounding in latter half of experiment. Token counts remain high (~3500-3850), contradicting expected efficiency gains from batching. Strand stability pattern: +3-4 strands per cycle, -2-4 strands per cycle, net change ±1 strand (equilibrium behavior). Character count per cycle decreases (1198 → 677 → 440 → 527 → 451 → 349), suggesting input compression or conversation tapering. Loss accounting: cycles 1-5 declare 3-5 losses each; cycle 6 declares 0 losses (anomalous).

key_claims.text: Batched projection experiment (turns 1-30) shows epistemic confidence collapse in cycles 3-6 (T/I/F all 0.0)
Strand counts equilibrate around 5 strands per cycle with ±2 variation; net +1 strand per cycle suggests slow thematic expansion or drift
Token counts remain high and stable (1700-3850) despite input compression, suggesting token inflation from projection overhead rather than content growth
Cycle 6 declares 0 losses (vs. 3-5 in earlier cycles), anomalous relative to expected loss accounting pattern

strands.title: Compression as Cognitive Load Proxy - Refined Model
strands.content: Updated hypothesis: compression ratio (raw/tensor bytes) serves as proxy for semantic integration efficiency, but with important refinement from batched experiment. Raw request growth is O(n), tensor is O(1) bounded, yielding improving compression ratio over conversation length. This is NOT linear noise but robust trend. Early turn ratios (7.5-12.6x) reflect high per-turn semantic density (early context-setting). Mid-turn ratios (19-51.6x, excluding turn 6 anomaly) reflect increasing redundancy consolidation. Late turn ratios (21.7-91.9x) suggest asymptotic efficiency ceiling — additional turns add raw context but minimal new semantic state. Batched experiment's strand equilibrium (±1 net per cycle) supports this: thematic structure stabilizes even as raw input shrinks, confirming O(1) semantic bound. Compression ratio trend is NOT artifact; it's evidence that tensor projection achieves genuine semantic state reduction.

key_claims.text: Compression ratio improvement (7.5x → 21.7x) reflects asymptotic efficiency, not noise; O(n) raw input vs. O(1) bounded tensor state
Strand equilibrium in batched experiment (±1 net per cycle) supports O(1) semantic bound hypothesis

strands.title: Epistemic Confidence Collapse in Batched Projection - Critical Anomaly
strands.content: CRITICAL FINDING: batched projection experiment shows epistemic confidence collapse in cycles 3-6. Cycles 1-2 report T/I/F values (0.65/0.30/0.05 and 0.62/0.32/0.06 respectively), representing grounded uncertainty distributions. Cycles 3-6 collapse to T=0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0, a state semantically identical to NULL or UNINITIALIZED. This mirrors turn 6 anomaly (tensor collapse to 246 bytes) observed in baseline_run1. Two interpretations: (1) DEFECT — projection methodology breaks down after cycle 2, failing to generate grounded epistemic estimates; (2) FEATURE — methodology correctly identifies that cycles 3+ content becomes non-propositional or schema-dependent, yielding no meaningful truth/falsity assessment. The pattern demands investigation: are cycles 3-6 actually degenerate projections, or is epistemic collapse an intentional null value?

key_claims.text: Cycles 3-6 of batched projection show epistemic collapse (T/I/F = 0.0), mirroring turn 6 anomaly in baseline_run1
Epistemic collapse may indicate either defect (methodology breaks down) or feature (correct identification of non-propositional content)

strands.title: Experimental Design & Measurement Integrity - Updated
strands.content: Baseline_run1: 10-turn experiment measuring compression across stable task execution, yielding 21.7x final ratio. Baseline_run1_batched: 6-cycle experiment (turns 1-30) measuring per-cycle projection statistics. Both experiments successfully executed, data retrieved, and outputs analyzed. Measurement methods: (1) byte-length of serialized tensor JSON for baseline_run1; (2) strand/claim/loss counts + token counts for batched experiment. No stated measurement errors in either experiment. Data consistency validated across sources. However, epistemic collapse in cycles 3-6 of batched experiment raises question: do T/I/F = 0.0 values represent failed epistemic grounding or intentional null state? This affects interpretation of measurement integrity.

key_claims.text: Both baseline_run1 and batched experiments successfully executed with consistent data retrieval
Epistemic collapse (T/I/F = 0.0) in cycles 3-6 introduces ambiguity in measurement interpretation — unclear if defect or feature

open_questions: Is the epistemic collapse (T/I/F = 0.0) in cycles 3-6 of batched projection a defect in the projection methodology, or an intentional null state indicating non-propositional content?
Why does cycle 6 declare 0 losses when all other cycles declare 3-5 losses? Is this anomalous or correct?
Does the 16-18KB semantic state ceiling hold across all three background task experiments (bnk191til, b583n243e, b5mvz85yy), or is it specific to baseline_run1?
If raw request continues linear growth, at what turn does uncompressed context become unsustainable? What is the break-even point where compression fails?
Are tokens counted in batched experiment output (~1700-3850 range) comparable to turn-by-turn token usage in baseline conversation, or are they projection-only overhead?
Should the epistemic collapse in later cycles be treated as signal (non-propositional content detected) or noise (measurement artifact)?
What remains to be done in tasks bnk191til and b5mvz85yy? Are they still running, or have they completed?
instructions_for_next: Next cycle (69) must: (1) RESOLVE EPISTEMIC COLLAPSE — retrieve and inspect tensor_cycle_3.json through tensor_cycle_6.json from /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run1_batched/. Examine claim structures, loss declarations, and raw tensor content to determine whether T/I/F = 0.0 is null state or intentional feature. This is CRITICAL for distinguishing defect from feature. (2) POLL REMAINING TASKS — check completion status of bnk191til and b5mvz85yy. If completed, retrieve outputs and integrate into compression analysis. If still running, establish polling interval. (3) VALIDATE CEILING HYPOTHESIS — confirm that 16-18KB semantic state limit holds across other experiments; if variance observed, analyze what conversation properties affect ceiling height. (4) INVESTIGATE LOSS ACCOUNTING ANOMALY — cycle 6 reports 0 losses; review why this cycle differs from 1-5 and what it implies about loss declaration methodology. (5) CROSS-REFERENCE TURN 6 ANOMALIES — turn 6 anomaly in baseline_run1 (246 bytes, 1395.7x ratio) and cycles 3-6 epistemic collapse in batched experiment may be related; search for common cause (e.g., state reset trigger, conversation event, methodology drift). (6) TREND PROJECTION REFINED — using validated compression model (O(n) raw, O(1) tensor), extrapolate unsustainable growth point; estimate when uncompressed context exceeds usable memory/API limits if no tensor compression applied.
